metadata.description: Blocked HRF decorator for sustained stimulus modeling

semantic_description.purpose: Validate the block_hrf function that creates blocked hemodynamic responses by convolving HRFs with boxcar functions for sustained stimulus modeling
semantic_description.algorithm:
The block_hrf function creates a blocked hemodynamic response by convolving an existing HRF with a boxcar (rectangular) function. This models the neural response to sustained stimuli rather than brief impulses, common in block design fMRI experiments.
Mathematical behavior: - Boxcar convolution: f_blocked(t) = ∫₀ʷ f_original(t - τ) · g(τ) dτ - where g(τ) is the boxcar function [1 for 0 ≤ τ ≤ width, 0 otherwise] - Discrete approximation: f_blocked(t) ≈ Σᵢ f_original(t - τᵢ) · decay(τᵢ) · precision - Exponential decay: decay(τ) = exp(-ln(2) · τ / half_life) - Summation vs max: aggregate responses across block duration
semantic_description.mathematical_properties: Key properties to validate: 1. Convolution accuracy: proper integration over block width 2. Sampling precision effects: convergence with finer sampling 3. Exponential decay: correct half-life behavior when specified 4. Aggregation modes: summation vs maximum selection 5. Normalization: peak scaling when requested 6. Span adjustment: span_new = span_original + width 7. Multi-basis handling: matrix operations preservation
semantic_description.edge_cases: - Zero width: should equal original HRF (impulse response) - Very large width: should produce plateau-like behavior - Exponential decay with half-life: value should halve every half_life units - Multi-basis HRFs: matrix operations should be preserved

input.name: block_width
input.description: Width of the block function for convolution

input.name: eval_times
input.description: Time points for evaluating blocked HRF

check.location: abs(evaluate(block_hrf(HRF_SPMG1, width = 2.0, precision = 0.1), 8.0) - evaluate(block_hrf(HRF_SPMG1, width = 2.0, precision = 0.5), 8.0))
max(evaluate(block_hrf(HRF_SPMG1, width = 4.0, precision = 0.1, normalize = TRUE), seq(0, 34, 0.1)))

implementations.R:
# R reference implementation using boxcar convolution # Function: block_hrf(hrf, width, precision, half_life, summate, normalize) # Location: R/hrf_decorators.R
# Define input variables from test specification block_width <- 5.0 precision <- 0.1 eval_times <- c(0.0, 2.5, 5.0, 7.5, 10.0, 15.0, 20.0) half_life <- 4.0
# Create base HRFs for testing - use predefined HRF objects, not raw functions hrf_spm <- HRF_SPMG1
# Test basic block convolution blocked_spmg1 <- block_hrf(hrf_spm, width = block_width, precision = precision) blocked_values <- evaluate(blocked_spmg1, eval_times)
# Peak enhancement check original_peak <- max(evaluate(hrf_spm, seq(0, 30, 0.1))) blocked_peak <- max(evaluate(blocked_spmg1, seq(0, 35, 0.1))) peak_enhancement <- blocked_peak > original_peak
implementations.Python:
# Python implementation should follow semantic specification # Focus on mathematical behavior, not R code structure
import numpy as np from scipy import signal
def block_hrf(hrf, width, precision=0.1, half_life=None, summate=True, normalize=False): """ Create blocked HRF by convolving with boxcar function.
Args: hrf: Base HRF object or function width: Width of the block function precision: Sampling precision for convolution half_life: Optional exponential decay half-life summate: Use summation (True) or maximum (False) aggregation normalize: Whether to normalize peak to 1.0
Returns: Blocked HRF object """ # Create time grid for convolution t_conv = np.arange(0, width + precision, precision)
# Create boxcar function boxcar = np.ones_like(t_conv)
# Apply exponential decay if specified if half_life is not None: decay = np.exp(-np.log(2) * t_conv / half_life) boxcar *= decay
class BlockedHRF: def __init__(self, original_hrf, convolution_kernel, span_adjustment): self.original_hrf = original_hrf self.kernel = convolution_kernel self.span = original_hrf.span + span_adjustment self.nbasis = getattr(original_hrf, 'nbasis', 1) self.width = width
def evaluate(self, t): """Evaluate blocked HRF at time points t.""" t = np.asarray(t) result = np.zeros_like(t, dtype=float)
for i, time_pt in enumerate(t): # Compute convolution at this time point conv_times = time_pt - t_conv valid_mask = conv_times >= 0
if np.any(valid_mask): hrf_values = np.array([self.original_hrf.evaluate(ct) for ct in conv_times[valid_mask]]) kernel_values = boxcar[valid_mask]
if summate: result[i] = np.sum(hrf_values * kernel_values) * precision else: result[i] = np.max(hrf_values * kernel_values)
# Normalize if requested if normalize and np.max(result) > 0: result = result / np.max(result)
return result
return BlockedHRF(hrf, boxcar, width)
def evaluate(hrf, t): """Evaluate HRF at time points t.""" return hrf.evaluate(t)
def nbasis(hrf): """Get number of basis functions.""" return getattr(hrf, 'nbasis', 1)
